perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall not permit any of its Affiliates to, directly or indirectly, hire or solicit any employee of the Company or encourage any such employee to leave such employment or hire any such employee who has left such employment, except pursuant to a general solicitation which is not directed specifically to any such employees; provided, that nothing in this Section 1 shall prevent Kennedy or any of his Affiliates from hiring (i) any employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company before the Merger, (ii) any employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company after the Merger one-hundred eighty (180) days from the date of termination of employment; or (iii) Jesse Miramontes (a current employee of the Company) and any Kennedy family member (including, without limitation, Nancy Kennedy (wife) or John C. Kennedy, IV (son) who are currently employed by the Company).
perturbation.changed_text: During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall not permit any of its Affiliates to, directly or indirectly, hire or solicit any employee of the Company or encourage any such employee to leave such employment; provided, however, Kennedy may hire any employee of the Company if that employee initiates the contact and applies for the position without direct solicitation from Kennedy or his affiliates. Kennedy can hire (i) any employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company before the Merger or (ii) any employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company after the Merger one-hundred eighty (180) days from the date of termination of employment.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "except pursuant to a general solicitation which is not directed specifically to any such employees" and adding "provided, however, Kennedy may hire any employee of the Company if that employee initiates the contact and applies for the position without direct solicitation from Kennedy or his affiliates," a loophole is created. While Kennedy cannot directly solicit employees, he can hire them if they apply on their own, even if influenced by indirect means. This contradicts the original intent of preventing Kennedy from actively recruiting Company employees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall not permit any of its Affiliates to, directly or indirectly, hire or solicit any employee of the Company or encourage any such employee to leave such employment; provided, however, Kennedy may hire any employee of the Company if that employee initiates the contact and applies for the position without direct solicitation from Kennedy or his affiliates. Kennedy can hire (i) any employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company before the Merger or (ii) any employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company after the Merger one-hundred eighty (180) days from the date of termination of employment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original restriction on Kennedy hiring employees of the Company. The original text prohibited Kennedy from hiring or soliciting Company employees, with limited exceptions. The changed text introduces a loophole allowing Kennedy to hire employees who initiate contact and apply without direct solicitation. The explanation clarifies that this creates a contradiction by allowing indirect recruitment, undermining the original intent. Thus, this loophole contradicts the initial restriction on hiring/soliciting employees of the Company and weakens the original intention of the clause, which is also expressed in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall not permit any of his Affiliates to, directly or indirectly, solicit or entice, or attempt to solicit or entice, any clients or customers of the Company or potential clients or customers of the Company for purposes of diverting their business or services from the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall not permit any of his Affiliates to, directly or indirectly, solicit or entice, or attempt to solicit or entice, any clients or customers of the Company for purposes of diverting their business or services from the Company, unless such clients or customers independently seek out Kennedy's services without any prior contact or encouragement from Kennedy or his affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "unless such clients or customers independently seek out Kennedy's services without any prior contact or encouragement from Kennedy or his affiliates" creates an exception that directly contradicts the restriction on soliciting or enticing clients. It allows Kennedy to work with Company clients if they initiate contact, undermining the non-solicitation clause. It becomes unclear what constitutes prohibited 'enticement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall not permit any of his Affiliates to, directly or indirectly, solicit or entice, or attempt to solicit or entice, any clients or customers of the Company or potential clients or customers of the Company for purposes of diverting their business or services from the Company, unless such clients or customers independently seek out Kennedy's services without any prior contact or encouragement from Kennedy or his affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the non-solicitation clause, allowing Kennedy to work with Company clients if they initiate contact. This directly contradicts the original restriction in both the Original Text and the Contradicted Text, which states that Kennedy shall not permit affiliates to solicit or entice clients for the purpose of diverting business. The addition undermines the purpose of the non-solicitation agreement as it makes it unclear what the original intent of the language. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Kennedy hereby represents that, except as he has disclosed in writing to Company, Kennedy is not bound by the terms of any agreement with any previous employer or other party to refrain from (i) competing, directly or indirectly, with the business of such previous employer or any other party or (ii) using or disclosing any trade secret or confidential or proprietary information in the course of the Restricted Period. Kennedy further represents that his performance of all the terms of this Agreement does not and will not breach any agreement to keep in confidence proprietary information, knowledge, or data acquired by Kennedy in confidence or in trust prior to the date of the Restricted Period, and Kennedy will not disclose to the Company or induce the Company to use any confidential or proprietary information or material belonging to any previous employer or others.
perturbation.changed_text: Kennedy hereby represents that, except as he has disclosed in writing to Company, Kennedy is not bound by the terms of any agreement with any previous employer or other party to refrain from (i) competing, directly or indirectly, with the business of such previous employer or any other party or (ii) using or disclosing any trade secret or confidential or proprietary information in the course of the Restricted Period. Kennedy represents to the best of his knowledge that his performance of all the terms of this Agreement does not and will not breach any agreement to keep in confidence proprietary information, knowledge, or data acquired by Kennedy in confidence or in trust prior to the date of the Restricted Period, and Kennedy will not knowingly disclose to the Company or induce the Company to use any confidential or proprietary information or material belonging to any previous employer or others.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Kennedy further represents that" to "Kennedy represents to the best of his knowledge that" and "Kennedy will not disclose to the Company" to "Kennedy will not knowingly disclose to the Company" weakens Kennedy's representation and creates uncertainty. The original text implied a strict, affirmative obligation. The modified text introduces a knowledge qualifier, meaning Kennedy is only liable if he *knows* he is breaching a prior agreement or disclosing confidential information, thus contradicting the strength of the previous statement. This means proving a breach becomes much harder, undermining the protective purpose of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Kennedy hereby represents that, except as he has disclosed in writing to Company, Kennedy is not bound by the terms of any agreement with any previous employer or other party to refrain from (i) competing, directly or indirectly, with the business of such previous employer or any other party or (ii) using or disclosing any trade secret or confidential or proprietary information in the course of the Restricted Period. Kennedy represents to the best of his knowledge that his performance of all the terms of this Agreement does not and will not breach any agreement to keep in confidence proprietary information, knowledge, or data acquired by Kennedy in confidence or in trust prior to the date of the Restricted Period, and Kennedy will not knowingly disclose to the Company or induce the Company to use any confidential or proprietary information or material belonging to any previous employer or others. Kennedy hereby indemnifies and agrees to defend and hold the Company harmless from and against any and all damages, liabilities, losses, costs, and expenses (including, without limitation, attorneys’ fees and the costs of investigation) resulting or arising directly or indirectly from any breach of the foregoing representations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens Kennedy's representation by adding a 'knowledge' qualifier ('to the best of his knowledge' and 'will not knowingly disclose'), where the original text imposed a strict obligation. This directly contradicts the indemnity clause in the contradicted text, which holds Kennedy liable for 'any breach of the foregoing representations'. The 'knowledge' qualifier makes it more difficult to prove a breach, thus undermining the indemnity clause and creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The Company acknowledges that Kennedy is engaged, and will continue to engage, in the manufacture and sale of medical devices (the “Medical Device Business”) utilizing processes, know-how and precision machining substantially similar to those utilized by the Company in connection with the Autocam Business. The Company agrees that nothing contained herein shall in any way limit Kennedy’s ability to engage in the Medical Device Business or to utilize such processes, know-how and precision machining in connection with such Medical Device Business.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company acknowledges that Kennedy is engaged, and will continue to engage, in the manufacture and sale of medical devices (the “Medical Device Business”) utilizing processes, know-how and precision machining substantially similar to those utilized by the Company in connection with the Autocam Business, provided that Kennedy’s activities in the Medical Device Business do not directly compete with specific product lines of the Autocam Business. The Company agrees that nothing contained herein shall in any way limit Kennedy’s ability to engage in the Medical Device Business or to utilize such processes, know-how and precision machining in connection with such Medical Device Business.
perturbation.explanation: Adding "provided that Kennedy’s activities in the Medical Device Business do not directly compete with specific product lines of the Autocam Business" creates a direct contradiction. The original clause states that *nothing* shall limit Kennedy's ability to engage in the Medical Device Business. The addition introduces a condition, limiting Kennedy's activities if they compete with Autocam's product lines, therefore, creates uncertainty about the limitations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company acknowledges that Kennedy is engaged, and will continue to engage, in the manufacture and sale of medical devices (the “Medical Device Business”) utilizing processes, know-how and precision machining substantially similar to those utilized by the Company in connection with the Autocam Business, provided that Kennedy’s activities in the Medical Device Business do not directly compete with specific product lines of the Autocam Business. The Company agrees that nothing contained herein shall in any way limit Kennedy’s ability to engage in the Medical Device Business or to utilize such processes, know-how and precision machining in connection with such Medical Device Business.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the condition "provided that Kennedy’s activities in the Medical Device Business do not directly compete with specific product lines of the Autocam Business." The original text states that "nothing contained herein shall in any way limit Kennedy’s ability to engage in the Medical Device Business." This creates a contradiction because the first statement says nothing limits Kennedy, but the added provision introduces a limitation based on competition, which is a direct conflict. This is a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction, as the added provision and the original clause in the same section are incompatible.
